name: Palencian Romanesque: Aguilar Reservoir Loop
name_original: Aguilar de Campoo desde Cervera de Pisuerga – circular por el Embalse de Aguilar
description: Cycle a moderate 41.3-mile circular route from Cervera de Pisuerga, exploring Aguilar de Campoo and the scenic Embalse de Aguilar.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cervera De Pisuerga navigate a landscape characterized by the Pisuerga River valley, the Aguilar Reservoir, and significant elevation changes leading to viewpoints and mountain passes. The region features varied terrain, from rolling hills to challenging ascents, often passing through areas with historical significance. Road cyclists can expect routes that traverse riverine environments and climb towards higher ground, offering diverse riding experiences. Best road cycling routes around Cervera De Pisuerga The most popular road cycling route is Palencian Romanesque: Aguilar Reservoir Loop, a 41.3 miles (66.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a significant climb and scenic views around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pisuerga River – Salinas de Pisuerga Bridge loop from Salinas de Pisuerga, a moderate 46.6 miles (75.1 km) path. This route follows the Pisuerga River, offering a mix of flat sections and gradual ascents. Local road cyclists also love the Salinas de Pisuerga Bridge – Alto del Bardal loop from Salinas de Pisuerga, a 53.6 miles (86.3 km) trail leading through river valleys and up to the Alto del Bardal, often completed in about 4 hours 11 minutes. Road cycling around Cervera De Pisuerga is defined by river valleys, reservoirs, and mountain passes, offering a range of challenges. The network provides options for easy road bike trails as well as more challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Cervera De Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1,100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cervera De Pisuerga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Cervera De Pisuerga, offering a diverse range of experiences for all skill levels. These routes navigate the Pisuerga River valley, the Aguilar Reservoir, and various mountain passes.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: Yes, Cervera De Pisuerga offers 3 easy road cycling routes. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and shorter distances, making them ideal for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. One popular moderate option that can be adapted for a less strenuous experience is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154675672/palencian-romanesque-aguilar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencian Romanesque: Aguilar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views without extreme climbs.

Q: What are the most challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Cervera De Pisuerga has 17 difficult road cycling routes. These often include significant elevation gains and longer distances. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31878467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera de Pisuerga – Historic center of Potes loop from Vado-Cervera&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 120 km with nearly 2000 meters of ascent, provide a demanding experience through mountainous terrain.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Cervera De Pisuerga are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pisuerga River – Salinas de Pisuerga Bridge loop from Salinas de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38179126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Pisuerga Bridge – Alto del Bardal loop from Salinas de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What kind of scenery or natural features can I expect on road cycling routes here?
A: Road cycling routes in Cervera De Pisuerga traverse a diverse landscape. You'll encounter the tranquil Pisuerga River valley, the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and ascents to various viewpoints and mountain passes. The region is characterized by rolling hills, dense forests, and open plains, often with views of the Cantabrian Mountains.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or lakes to see while road cycling?
A: Yes, the region boasts several beautiful natural water features. You might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3487275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mazobre Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or cycle alongside the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3936745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruesga Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. These offer picturesque spots for a break or photo opportunity.

Q: What are the best seasons for road cycling in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: The best seasons for road cycling in Cervera De Pisuerga are typically spring and autumn. During these months, the weather is mild, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, though some routes may be exposed to higher temperatures.

Q: What are the conditions like for road cycling in winter?
A: Winter road cycling in Cervera De Pisuerga can be challenging due to colder temperatures, potential snow or ice at higher elevations, and shorter daylight hours. While lower valley routes might remain accessible, mountain passes can be closed or require specialized equipment. It's advisable to check local weather and road conditions before planning a winter ride.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, there are easier sections, particularly along the river valleys, that can be suitable for families. Look for the 3 easy-rated routes, which generally have less traffic and flatter terrain, offering a safer and more enjoyable experience for all ages.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: The road cycling routes in Cervera De Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from challenging climbs to scenic reservoir loops, and the opportunity to explore the region's natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant elevation gain for a challenge?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Cervera De Pisuerga are known for their substantial elevation gains, making them perfect for cyclists looking to test their climbing abilities. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera de Pisuerga – Alto de La Varga Viewpoint loop from Vado-Cervera&lt;/a&gt; includes over 1100 meters of ascent, leading to rewarding panoramic views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, road cycling routes often pass by significant landmarks. You can cycle near the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2065701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar de Campoo Dam&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, or even spot distant peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra&lt;/a&gt;, which are prominent natural monuments in the area.
tips: Cervera de Pisuerga is a small tourist mountain town in the heart of the Palencia Mountain, part of the Cantabrian Mountains. The landscape and views will blow you away. An amazing place to bike and explore.

The Aguilar reservoir collects the snowmelt and rivers from the Montaña Palencia region in the province of Palencia. In addition to providing water and hydroelectric power to local communities, the reservoir's natural beauty creates the backdrop for your adventure. Stop for a swim on your next trip.

There are many things to see in Aguilar de Campoo. Apart from having several biscuit factories, it is also the town of the Romanesque, of the manor houses, shields and coats of arms. In fact, this town and its surroundings are home to the highest concentration of Romanesque monuments in all of Europe.

An old abbey of the Premonstratensian order, declared a National Monument in 1866. It is a place of symbolic importance in the Romanesque of Aguilar de Campoo, Palencia and all of Spain and is also the current headquarters of the Center for Romanesque Studies. The space is a real treasure and the guided tour is highly recommended.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered a moderate road ride. It covers approximately 66.5 kilometers with an elevation gain of around 510 meters, requiring good fitness. The terrain is predominantly well-paved, making for an enjoyable ride on smooth surfaces.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect mostly well-paved surfaces, ideal for race bikes. While the primary description emphasizes smooth roads, some segments classified as 'moderate' in the region might include short unpaved sections. Overall, it's designed for an enjoyable road cycling experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Cervera de Pisuerga. You should be able to find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient starting and ending point for this circular tour.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. Key highlights include the picturesque Aguilar Reservoir, the historic Aguilar de Campoo Castle and Romanesque Monuments, and the impressive Monastery of Santa María la Real de Aguilar de Campoo. You'll also pass through diverse landscapes of forests, mountains, and charming towns with Romanesque art.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty, approximately 66.5 km distance, and 510 meters of elevation gain, this route is best suited for cyclists with good fitness and some experience. While the terrain is mostly paved, it might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montaña Palentina region is beautiful throughout the warmer months. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for cycling. Summer can also be good, but be prepared for potentially higher temperatures.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Cervera de Pisuerga and Aguilar de Campoo offer various services, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. You'll find opportunities to refuel and rest in these towns.

faqs.question: Does this route pass through any natural parks or protected areas?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route takes you through the heart of the Palentina Mountains, including areas within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park and the Las Loras UNESCO World Geopark. These areas are known for their impressive natural settings and rich biodiversity.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads and trails within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, which are generally accessible without charge. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, primarily on paved surfaces. While dogs are generally allowed in the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, bringing a dog on a racebike route of this length and difficulty might not be practical or safe for the animal. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, on a leash when appropriate, and consider its endurance for such a long ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Camino Olvidado (Matamorisca-Puente Almuhey), Camino Natural del Románico Palentino. Alternativa 2: Perazancas de Ojeda a Dehesa de Montejo, and the Románico Palentino trail.
